channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - kotaku.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for kotaku.com

item.title: Monster Hunter Wilds Reveals The Elder Dragons Are Coming In A New Expansion And, Surprise, There’s A Switch 2 Port In The Works
item.content:encoded:
Monster Hunter Wilds‘ big expansion has been revealed and it’s called Ascendance. It’s taking players to the sky and pitting them against Elder Dragons. It’s arriving sometime in 2027. Oh, there’s also a Switch 2 port in development.

Capcom is calling Ascendance a “massive” expansion, which should mean it will essentially feel like Monster Hunter Wilds 2.0. That ambitious scope certainly feels apparent in the DLC’s first trailer which shows hunters ditching the Forbidden Lands for the floating ruins of an ancient Wyverian civilization in the sky called the Skybound Eyrie. “Elder Dragons are coming,” warns Capcom. “Prepare yourselves!”

As with all big Monster Hunter expansions, Ascendance will bring not just a new location but also new abilities, gear, and new legendary monsters to hunt in Master Rank. Here’s a look:

In a developer interview, expansion director Takuro Hiraoka called out some of the specific elements that will set the expansion apart from the base game. One of those is the floating islands themselves, which let players ride updrafts on their mounts to navigate the landscape’s new verticality.

Another big addition is the Booster Bracer, a new bracelet on their hunter’s wrist that players can activate to imbue their attacks and abilities with extra power. It’ll also give a players greater mobility in a pinch, helping them react more quickly to the tougher enemies they’ll be facing in the expansion. Those include the massive Elder Dragons like Kushala Daora and several others, in addition to other monsters stalking the Skybound Eyrie.

Capcom also quietly revealed that Monster Hunter Wilds is in development for Switch 2. The long-rumored port doesn’t have a release date yet, but it seems safe to assume that Ascendance will be part of that version of the game too, at least eventually. How exactly is Capcom going to get a game that’s struggled with performance even on PC to feel and look good on Nintendo’s new portable? I have no idea, but it’ll be cool to see the latest mainline entry in the franchise expand to a new platform and hopefully bring with it cross-play and cross-progression.



https://kotaku.com/monster-hunter-wilds-reveals-the-elder-dragons-are-coming-in-a-new-expansion-and-surprise-theres-a-switch-2-port-2000703218
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Godzilla Is The Only One Not Afraid Of GTA 6 As Another November Release Date Finally Leaks
item.content:encoded:
Grand Theft Auto VI has been scaring away basically every other major publisher from releasing a game in November 2026. Except the rights holder to a cult-hit 2002 GameCube fighting game. According to a new leak, a remaster of Godzilla: Destroy All Monsters Melee will arrive just a couple weeks earlier. Finally, something else to play that month.

Billbil-kun at Dealabs Magazine reports that the the currently unannounced game will arrive on November 3 for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series X/S, and Switch 2. It’s being developed by Pipeworks and published by Atari, and will arrive with some new bells and whistles. Those include a new online multiplayer mode, overhauled graphics and unlock systems, and additional single-player story content. 

If you’ve never played Godzilla: Destroy All Monsters Melee, it’s essentially a game where you rampage through big retro-futuristic cities fighting other monsters and save Earth while also destroying it. It lives fondly  in the memories of people who once played it at their friend’s house or rented it from blockbuster, and was actually nominated for fighting game of the year before losing out, understandably, to Tekken 4.

“Name one bad thing about Godzilla Destroy All Monsters Melee?” someone wrote on the Godzilla subreddit less than a year ago. “That it’s not remastered yet,” read the top response. The power of posting strikes again.

GTA 6 is the only video game releasing in November

No one wants to launch anywhere near it 💀 pic.twitter.com/BKdmtCLCyQ

— GTA 6 Intel (@GTA6Intel) June 3, 2026

The other notable thing about the leaked remaster, however, is that it’s daring to encroach upon the release month of Grand Theft Auto VI when no one else has dared to. Well, except for Gothic 3 Classic. That remaster is arriving on November 24, a few days after GTA VI‘s November 19 launch.

Will Godzilla be help shatter the damn on other notable games announcing November release dates? Or perhaps Godzilla Destroy All Monsters Melee will simply scare them away even more.



https://kotaku.com/godzilla-is-the-only-one-not-afraid-of-gta-6-as-another-november-release-date-finally-leaks-2000703439
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: A Beloved Final Fantasy 7 Character Is Getting Rebuilt From The Ground Up For Street Fighter 6
item.content:encoded:
Final Fantasy VII‘s Tifa Lockhart will be Street Fighter 6‘s latest guest character. The RPG martial arts powerhouse is joining as part of the  Capcom fighting game’s year 4 slate of new characters. She’ll even be arriving with a unique magic system to augment her move set.

“Tifa is a character loved by players all around the world,” Final Fantasy VII director Naoki Hamaguchi said on stage during her Summer Game Fest reveal. “That’s exactly why this was done to bring to life a Tifa who truly fights within the world of Street Fighter 6.”

According to the fighting game’s director, Takayuki Nakayama, the Avalanche member will be arriving with her Materia powers “reimagined as a new gameplay system. “Everything from her design to movement has been rebuilt as Tifa the Street Fighter,” he said.

Here’s the reveal teaser. It doesn’t show Tifa’s character model or what she looks like in action. Those bits will be revealed later on, Nakayma said.

Street Fighter 6‘s year 4 roster expansion will start getting added to the game this summer. The other characters are World Tour protagonist Bosch and two fighters who are completely new to the series: Yasmine and Arjun. Yasmine will be introduced on August 3, followed by Arjun in the fall, Tifa in “early” 2027, and Bosch later on that spring.

This isn’t the first time Tifa has appeared in a fighting game. Back during the PS1-era, both her and Cloud appeared in Ehrgeiz. Since then, Cloud has appeared in Super Smash Bros. Ultimate, and both Noctis (FFXV) and Clive (FFXVI) appeared in the Tekken franchise. In fact, Tekken 8 fans have been begging to have Tifa added to the Bandai Namco fighter for years.

“We all know she is attractive and I understand that,” Tekken creator Katsuhiro Harada said back in 2024. “But as yet we have not decided anything about guest characters etc.” He has since left to start-up his own studio. “Harada quit Tekken because he couldn’t get Tifa,” joked one Tekken fan on the subreddit after today’s announcement.



https://kotaku.com/a-beloved-final-fantasy-7-character-is-getting-rebuilt-from-the-ground-up-for-street-fighter-6-2000703222
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
